serviceDescription: Spatial data is collected by the National Disaster Recovery Team from each unit point of contact. The spatial locations are pulled from fire occurrence and perimeter datasets, FEMA, States, and local unit disaster response assessments. The polygons are then processed through intersections with geopolitical boundaries such as states, counties, and congressional districts as well as administrative boundaries such as regions and forests.

documentInfo.Subject: This is an agency-wide disaster recovery geospatial dataset that will support communications and stakeholder engagement, delineate where disaster recovery projects align with other priorities such as active management and recreation. The data in this dataset supports standardized reporting and resource optimization.
